pattern: Every split's total cost is the two outer weights plus one boundary weight-pair sum for each internal cut, so the spread between the best and worst split reduces to choosing which boundary pairs to pick.
hints: Any way of splitting into k groups always includes the first and last weight in the total, plus the sum of the two weights straddling each of the k - 1 internal cut points.
So the total cost of a split is completely determined by which k - 1 adjacent-pair sums you use as cut points, out of all the adjacent pairs available.
To maximize, pick the k - 1 largest adjacent-pair sums; to minimize, pick the k - 1 smallest; the answer is the difference between those two totals.

edge_inputs.description: a small array split into two bags
k equals the array length, only one distribution is possible
k = 1, the whole array is one bag
k equals the array length with more elements
k is one less than the array length
identical weights give every adjacent pair the same sum
weights at both extremes of the allowed range
a zigzag weight pattern with clearly distinct largest and smallest boundary pairs

stress.description: a large array of random weights near the maximum size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    weights = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [weights, k]
